body_txt: Money makes life go around, and lots of people desire to be famous (hence the proliferation of Reality TV shows and social media "influencers"). It seems only a complete imbecile would throw away a shot at money or fame. However, characters who get their fame often regret it due to the problems it brings, and in certain series can even end up rejecting whatever brings them celebrity and wealth to embrace a more private lifestyle, even if it requires them to lose money as well. See All That Glitters and Wanting Is Better Than Having . Named for a line in Rocket Power 's "Reggie's Big (Beach) Break"... which isn't quite an example of this trope, even though the button does get pushed. A variation is when the characters don't necessarily mind being famous so much; it's what they're famous for — i.e., something they're embarrassed or ashamed of — which is the problem. Can result in Angst Dissonance if not handled carefully; perhaps unsurprisingly, people who aren't rich and famous but would very much like to be tend to react poorly when people who are rich and famous complain about it. That said, this trope can be Truth in Television : having every aspect of your life scrutinized, getting castigated for every little mistake, being unable to go anywhere in public with peace and quiet, the Paparazzi and Loony Fan stalkers, and the general sense that most people either want something from you or pettily envy you . This trope can lead someone to become a Reclusive Artist . Sister Trope to Familial Fame Frustrations . Compare Horrible Hollywood , Lonely at the Top , Gracefully Demoted and Ambition Is Evil . Not to be confused with Hype Backlash , which is when significant praise for a piece of media leads to some people being disappointed with it.
